sections.paragraph:
ἐλεεινός, ή, όν (s. ἔλεος; Hom. et al.; Diod S 13, 28, 3; Dio Chrys. 33 [50], 3 and 4; Ael. Aristid. 34, 47 K.=50 p. 564 D.; Philostrat., Imag. 1, 4 p. 300, 6; POxy 130, 3; 131, 2; TestSol 2:13 D; ApcEsdr 2:30 p. 26, 24 Tdf.; ApcSed; Jos., Ant. 4, 133, Vi. 138; Mel., P. 27, 192 [Ch.; ἐλεινόν PBodm for Att. ἐλεινός; so W-H.].—B-D-F §31, 3; Rob. 204; Mussies 24) pert. to being deserving of sympathy for one’s pathetic condition, miserable, pitiable (w. ταλαίπωρος) Rv 3:17. Comp. ἐλεεινότεροι πάντων ἀνθρώπων as superl. (s. B-D-F §60; Mlt. 78f) most miserable of all 1 Cor 15:19.—DELG s.v. ἔλεος. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

PBodm PBodm = Papyrus Bodmer, s. P66; P72; P74; P75—List 1

Att. Att. = Attic, esp. in ref. to dialect

W-H. W-H. = BWestcott/FHort—List 1, beg.

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Mussies Mussies = GM., The Morphology of Koine Greek—List 6

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

superl. superl. = superlative

Mlt. Mlt. = JMoulton; with page number alone specified, this abbr. refers to A Grammar of NT Greek I: Prolegomena—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 315
